title: Design-token set
description: The machine-readable design-token artifact produced by the design-spec-architect agent alongside STYLE_TILE.md and UI_STYLE_GUIDE.md. Two tiers: primitives (raw scales, no meaning) and semantic tokens (roles a component references, each aliasing a primitive or literal). Downstream frontend build tickets generate the theme (Tailwind config / CSS custom properties) from this file and lint built UI against it. DTCG-adjacent, deliberately lighter. The guide's token section is the human mirror of this file.

version.description: Schema version of this file. Bump only on breaking schema changes.

spec.description: Filename of the companion style guide (e.g. UI_STYLE_GUIDE.md).

mode.description: Where this token set's authority comes from: 'invention' (derived from the product spec's brand intent) or 'reference' (extracted from a controlling reference — brand guide, existing design system, reference repo/site; see design-spec-standards.md). In reference mode, semantic tokens are expected to carry `source` provenance.

references.description: In reference mode: the controlling sources in precedence order (e.g. 'brand-guide.pdf', 'acme-web/tailwind.config.js', 'https://stripe.com').

primitives.description: Tier 1: the raw scales, no meaning attached. A mapping of category (e.g. color, space, font-size) to a mapping of primitive name to literal value.

tokens.description: Tier 2: the flat list of semantic tokens — the roles components reference. Components reference these only, never primitives or raw values.

key.description: Stable, unique, kebab-case identifier for the semantic role (e.g. color-primary, text-muted, radius-card). This is the identity components and theme generators reference.

category.description: The token's kind, which routes it in theme generation.

value.description: Either a primitive alias of the form '{primitives.<category>.<name>}' (preferred — the validator checks it resolves) or a literal value. Color literals/resolutions must be hex (#rgb/#rrggbb) so contrast is computable.

description.description: The role in one line: where this token is used and what it means (e.g. 'Primary action / brand accent.').

source.description: Provenance (reference mode): where this value was extracted from ('brand-guide §3.2', 'acme-web/tailwind.config.js:14') or how a gap was filled ('derived: brand ratio', 'invented: no reference'). Expected on every semantic token in reference mode.

contrastsWith.description: For color tokens: the key of the token this one must remain legible against (e.g. text-default contrastsWith color-surface). Declares a load-bearing pair; the validator computes the real WCAG ratio from resolved hex.

minContrast.description: The WCAG contrast ratio the contrastsWith pair must meet. Defaults to 4.5 (AA normal text) when contrastsWith is present; use 3 for large text / essential UI boundaries.

tracesTo.description: Optional PR-* product-requirement IDs this token serves (the traceability spine). Each must look like a PR-* id; the validator checks existence when any tracesTo is used.

allOf.description: minContrast only makes sense on a declared pair.
